result:
**Goal: Add a Blood Vessel Network & Angiogenesis simulation mode.**

A pulsing vascular network that grows itself through angiogenesis — vessel tip cells sprout toward VEGF (vascular endothelial growth factor) gradients secreted by oxygen-starved tissue, branching and anastomosing into a self-organizing capillary network. Pulsatile blood flow driven by a heartbeat pressure wave delivers oxygen to surrounding tissue; vessels remodel by Murray's law (high-flow vessels widen, unused ones regress). A toggleable tumor mode lets a growing tumor hijack the vasculature by flooding VEGF, recruiting its own blood supply. Three views: (1) living vessel network with flowing red blood cells, pulsing arteries, sprouting tip cells, and optional tumor mass, (2) oxygen/VEGF heatmap showing tissue perfusion and hypoxic zones, (3) time-series sparklines for metrics like perfusion efficiency, vessel density, flow rate, oxygen coverage, VEGF levels, and tumor size. Six presets spanning healthy tissue growth, wound healing, tumor angiogenesis, anti-angiogenic drug therapy, retinal vasculature, and exercise-induced capillarization.

This directly fills a gap — the project has no cardiovascular or medical-biology simulation — and it's a natural complement to the existing biological modes. The visual of a beating vascular tree with flowing blood cells growing in real time will be striking in the terminal.
